rationale: Near-total mismatch: the high-intensity anime combat aesthetic (saturated blue/gold/black, shounen power fantasy, aggressive "try and survive" challenge framing) is the polar opposite of Marble Sort's pastel, calm, satisfying sorting loop targeted at casual women 25-55. There is no character in the game, no combat, and no way to authentically translate a Vegito energy-charge hook into conveyor-belt color sorting without a jarring bait-and-switch that will tank IPM and retention quality. Skip this archetype; if a "challenge" emotional pitch is desired, test a softer "Can you sort this in time?" failure-bait variant in the game's native pastel palette instead.
